title: Executive Order 9296—Amending Paragraph 1 of Title II of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941 Concerning Reports of Contracts and Purchases Made Under Authority of Title II of the First War Powers Act, 1941

flags.justification: This modifies a reporting mechanism, not the underlying contracting authority itself.

The original EO 9001 required quarterly reports to the President covering all contracts and purchases; this amendment replaces that requirement with a duty merely to 'maintain complete data,' with public availability left to each department's own determination of what is 'compatible with the public interest' — a real weakening relative to the original baseline.
A reporting-mechanism amendment, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A narrow procedural amendment to an already time-bound wartime framework.

The removal of the specific quarterly report-to-President requirement, replaced with less structured data maintenance and agency-discretion public disclosure, is a real, if narrow, reduction in the original order's oversight and reporting frequency.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A narrow, technical amendment to a reporting mechanism.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 2/10 = 20.0%. A second confirmed instance of the 'quiet regression' pattern first identified in EO 12072: a technical amendment that weakens rather than strengthens or maintains an existing accountability mechanism, here specifically reducing EO 9001's original quarterly presidential reporting requirement to agency-discretion data maintenance.
